title: tragacantha bactriana
intro: Tragacantha bactriana, also known as Russian knapweed or Bactrian needle grass, is a perennial plant in the family Asteraceae, native to Central Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Tragacantha bactriana include relief from diarrhea, dysentery, and fever, as well as its potential to reduce the risk of certain gastrointestinal diseases. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and decoctions, are typically made by drying the roots and leaves, which can then be used as a tea or added to other remedies.
